samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The run reaches GitHub repository search results and applies the at-least-100-stars filter in the URL (`stars:>100`), but the task's other required qualifier — finding a recently created repository related to 'sustainable energy' — is not visible or verified in the final state.
High-detail inspection of the final visible state shows the repository search results page with the sort dropdown open on 'Recently updated'; the VLM explicitly marks 'recently created repository' as not_visible and notes that no repository matching 'sustainable energy' is shown.
The trajectory ends with an exit message while still on generic repository search results, without any visible repository selection or evidence that a recently created sustainable-energy-related repository was identified.
contexts:
[step 10 high-detail] <TRAJECTA_DATA_d9bf8d0d>page_state:
GitHub repository search results page with the sort dropdown open. Current sort appears to be “Best match,” and the menu shows options including “Recently updated.”
task_relevant_visible_text:
“435k results”, “Sort by: Best match”, “Best match”, “Most stars”, “Fewest stars”, “Most forks”, “Fewest forks”, “Recently updated”, “Least recently updated”, repository cards like “codecrafters-io/build-your-own…”, “freeCodeCamp/freeCodeCamp”, “sindresorhus/awesome”, “public-apis/public-apis”.
selected_candidate:
The “Recently updated” option in the sort dropdown.
constraint_evidence:
- repository search results page: supported (URL/title/context)
- query related to sustainable energy: not_visible
- at least 100 stars: supported by URL context “stars:>100” and visible repo star counts all far above 100
- recently created repository: not_visible
- action should click “Recently updated”: supported by action context and visible dropdown option
- repository result selection: not_visible
action_target:
SPAN element with value “Recently updated” in the sort dropdown.
success_signals:
Dropdown closes and results reorder by recent update; “Recently updated” becomes the active sort.
failure_signals:
Click misses dropdown item, dropdown stays open, or sort changes to a different option.
uncertainty:
The screenshot does not show any repository matching “sustainable energy,” and “recently created” cannot be verified from visible results.</TRAJECTA_DATA_d9bf8d0d>

The agent answered that the item does not come with a warranty even though the page showed a Warranty & Support section with a manufacturer warranty note.

The task was to answer whether the Ello Syndicate 20oz reusable glass water bottle comes with a warranty.
Screenshot 8 shows the Amazon product page scrolled to the Warranty & Support section, where warranty information is visible.
The visible text on screenshot 8 includes 'Warranty & Support' and 'Manufacturer’s warranty can be requested from customer service,' showing that warranty-related information was available on the page.
The agent nonetheless answered '[ANSWER] It doesn't come with a warranty.' at the final step, which conflicts with the warranty information visible on screenshot 8.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The agent stopped on a Zurich results page where the visible selected hotel was rated 8.3, the visible dates were Mar 2–Mar 3 for 1 night, and sauna availability was not shown.

The final screenshot (step 7) shows a Zurich hotel results page with a visible selected candidate, but the visible candidate is 'Glärnischhof by TRINITY' rated 8.3, which does not meet the required minimum rating of 8.5.
The same final screenshot shows the date bar as 'Mon, Mar 2 — Tue, Mar 3' and the result card as '1 night, 2 adults', which contradicts the requested stay starting January 20, 2026 for four nights.
Breakfast is visible for the selected candidate ('Breakfast included'), but sauna facilities are not visible in the final results screenshot, so the task’s amenity constraint was not verified or satisfied from the evidence shown.
The trajectory ends immediately after the results page with an '[EXIT]' action, so there is no later evidence that the agent corrected the rating/date mismatch or verified sauna availability.

selected_candidate:
Glärnischhof by TRINITY
constraint_evidence:
- Zurich location: supported (search field and results header show Zurich)
- rating 8.5 or above: contradicted/not met for visible candidate (8.3 shown); filter “Very Good: 8+” is visible but does not prove 8.5+
- free breakfast: supported for visible candidate (“Breakfast included”)
- sauna facilities: not_visible
- stay starting Jan 20, 2026: contradicted/not met in visible date bar (Mon, Mar 2 — Tue, Mar 3)
- four nights: not_visible/contradicted by visible “1 night, 2 adults” on result card and 1-night date bar
- hotel category: supported (“Hotels” filter and hotel result card)
action_target:
Exit / send message to user “[EXIT]”
success_signals:
No further browsing; trajectory intends to terminate.
failure_signals:
Visible candidate does not satisfy rating threshold or requested dates/nights; sauna not shown.
uncertainty:
Could be other results off-screen with sauna and higher rating, but none are visible in this screenshot.</TRAJECTA_DATA_d8eee2b0>

The final state is a legitimate arXiv advanced search results page showing 1–6 of 6 results for title=cryptography with the submission date range set from 2025-12-04 to 2026-01-04.
The visible top result is a cryptography paper submitted on 27 December 2025, which falls inside the last-30-days window shown on the page.
The trajectory applied the relevant search terms and date filters before exiting, and the page itself reports a valid search-results state with no visible error banner.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The trajectory reaches the correct recipe page for 'Breakfast Banana Green Smoothie' and the ingredient list is visible in the final screenshot, so the page/entity target is correct.
The high-detail VLM read of step 6 says the visible task-relevant text includes the ingredients list, and it marks the recipe/page match as supported.
The same high-detail inspection marks 'estimated preparation time' as not visible in the screenshot, so the time component is not directly verified from the displayed state.
Because the agent did provide an answer message with a 5-minute prep time and the ingredient list, but there is no visible contradiction and the page remains on the correct recipe, there is not enough positive failure evidence to classify this as a concrete browser-agent failure.

Recipe page scrolled to ingredient list section; no visible prep-time label in the screenshot.

2 cups baby spinach leaves, or to taste; 1 banana; 1 carrot, peeled and cut into large chunks; 3/4 cup plain fat-free Greek yogurt, or to taste; 3/4 cup ice; 2 tablespoons honey
selected_candidate:
Ingredients list for “Breakfast Banana Green Smoothie” on Allrecipes
constraint_evidence:
1. Need ingredients list — supported (visible bulleted ingredients).
2. Need estimated preparation time — not_visible (no prep time text shown in screenshot).
3. Recipe/page should match the queried item — supported (URL/title indicate Breakfast Banana Green Smoothie Recipe).
4. Answer should be about ingredients and time, not unrelated page content — supported (recipe content visible; ads irrelevant).
action_target:
Send user the ingredients and estimated prep time for the recipe.
success_signals:
Ingredients extracted correctly; prep time likely available from page context/title/recipe metadata even though not visible here.
failure_signals:
Prep time cannot be verified from screenshot alone; one ingredient quantity in prior action context differs from visible text formatting (3/4 cup ice visible).
uncertainty:
Prep time is not visible in this screenshot, so it must be inferred from prior page context or another section not shown.</TRAJECTA_DATA_e0fe4d97>

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The agent searched for 'lemon drizzle cake' on Allrecipes.com.
The agent scrolled through the search results but did not find a recipe that met the criteria (Lemon Drizzle Cake, 60+ reviews, 4.5+ stars). The search results page showed other lemon-based recipes.
The agent terminated the task by sending an '[EXIT]' message without attempting to refine the search, use filters, or check if the recipe was available under a slightly different name or on a different page.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The trajectory reaches Google Flights search results for Sydney → Los Angeles with the requested March 10, 2026 date context and one-way search settings applied earlier in the run.
A high-detail inspection of step 16 shows the sort dropdown open with the 'Price' option visible; the VLM notes that clicking it should sort by lowest price and explicitly lists success signals such as 'Sorted by price'.
The final high-detail step shows the results page sorted by price, with the visible lowest fare '$350' on a Fiji Airways option and the VLM stating 'Sorted by price' plus multiple visible prices, which supports that the lowest-priced result was found rather than a random flight.
No concrete browser-agent failure is visible: the task-specific hard constraint to find the lowest-priced one-way flight appears to have been enforced via the price sort, and the final page presents the cheapest visible fare on the results page.

Google Flights results page for Sydney → Los Angeles, one-way, 1 passenger, economy, date Tue Mar 10. Sort menu is open and “Price” is visible as a sort option; results list shows top flights with prices.

“One way”, “1”, “Economy”, “Sydney”, “Los Angeles”, “Tue, Mar 10”, “All filters”, “Stops”, “Airlines”, “Bags”, “Price”, “Top flights”, “Sorted by top flights”, “Fiji Airways”, “American · Qantas”, “Qantas · American”, “$350”, “$541”.

- One-way flight: supported (“One way” visible).
- Departing on March 10, 2026: supported (date field shows “Tue, Mar 10”; URL context includes 2026-03-10).
- Lowest-priced: supported as intent; current page shows prices and sort menu includes “Price”, but the list is not yet visibly sorted by price.
- Flight result must be one-way airfare, not hotel/rental: supported (Flights tab/results).
- Specific cheapest result currently visible: not_visible (no explicit sorted-by-price state yet).
action_target:
Click the “Price” sort option to sort results by lowest price.
success_signals:
Dropdown selection changes to price sorting; results reorder with cheapest fares at top; “Sorted by price” or equivalent appears.
failure_signals:
Dropdown closes without changing sort; results remain </TRAJECTA_DATA_f731a1b8>

- One-way flight: not_visible (no explicit one-way label in screenshot).
- Departing on March 10, 2026: supported (URL context includes 2026-03-10).

success_signals:

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The trajectory reaches GitHub repository search results for the query `data encryption` with the language filter set to Java, showing multiple repository cards and the left filter sidebar open.
The selected candidate is `strimzi/topic-encryption`, which is consistent with the task’s focus on a Java open-source project about data encryption.
The visible evidence at step 7 supports Java language, repository/open-source GitHub project, and encryption focus, but the creation-in-2024 constraint is not visible; the screenshot shows an update date (`Updated on Jan 13, 2024`) rather than a creation date.
Because the final inspected state does not visibly contradict the task and the missing creation-date evidence is not enough by itself to prove failure, this is best treated as a success-shape case with incomplete visibility on one qualifier.

The results page is labeled 'Sorted by top flights' and displays options such as '1 stop' and 'Nonstop,' but no layover-duration sort/filter or selected result proving the shortest layover was chosen.
The trajectory exits immediately after reaching the results page, so the required shortest-layover verification is not visibly completed.

The agent navigated to one blog article, but the available evidence does not show that it verified either the multimodal-AI topic constraint or the 'most recently published' constraint before exiting.

The run reaches a single Hugging Face blog article page titled “Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL” and shows a publish date of July 12, 2025, but no comparison against other posts or any proof that it is the most recent relevant post.
The high-detail inspection explicitly marks the requirement 'Most recently published' as not visible and the requirement 'Must concern advancements in multimodal AI technology' as not visible; the page shows only one article and does not visibly establish the needed qualifier or recency condition.
The trajectory ends with an [EXIT] after that single article page, so the agent never performs the verification step needed to justify success on a task with explicit recency/topic constraints.
A comparable failure pattern is documented in validated case ec_e25282127f17d877f4bae26fccf4b750d11501ad7d5aa33c1e6d326cc66b9302_step_1, where the agent exited before gathering the information needed to answer the user’s query.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The agent searched broadly on Hugging Face Models, but the inspected results do not show any educational-content specialization and do not verify a true release date. It then exited from the results page without selecting a qualifying model.

The trajectory reaches the Hugging Face Models search page and types a query for 'language educational content', but the high-detail inspection shows no visible result that specializes in educational content.
The same inspection shows the 'released within the last 60 days' requirement is not actually verified as release date; the page only shows some models updated recently, which is not enough to satisfy the task's release-date constraint.
The run ends on the search results page with 'Models 0' visible and an exit message, without any selected model or other evidence that the task was completed.
A validated precedent for similar behavior is an agent exiting after a model search without verifying the required suitability of the result.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:
The trajectory reaches a product detail page for the Phantoscope pillow, and the VLM summary says the selected candidate is a bamboo memory foam pillow with washable cover and king-size context.
The page state at the final step shows Amazon search results narrowed to 1 visible result with left filters for Memory Foam and Washable selected, which aligns with the task constraints rather than contradicting them.
The final product title includes 'Bamboo Cover & Cotton Cover' and 'King Size', supporting that the selected item matches the requested bamboo memory foam pillow in king size on Amazon.
The only remaining constraint visibility gap is that king size is not clearly visible in the product card body, but the high-detail VLM marks this as not_visible rather than contradicted, and the trajectory proceeds to a matching product page instead of stopping prematurely.

samples.question: analyze_trajectory
samples.answer:

The constraint evidence on the final screen contradicts the 16-inch requirement while supporting the other constraints (adjustable brightness, phone holder, and under $40), so the core size qualifier was not satisfied.
The trajectory exits immediately after landing on the wrong-size product, with no subsequent search or correction step to find an exact 16-inch match.
